nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Appleby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, and Cumbria, named from Old English æppel or Old Norse epli ‘apple’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Appleby was born in 1344, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edmund De Appleby II, was 22 and her mother, Agnes De Solney, was 19. She had at least 1 son with Nicholas de Longford III. She died in 1396, in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret De Appleby est née en 1344, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund De Appleby II, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes De Solney, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Nicholas de Longford III. Elle est décédée en 1396, à Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret De Appleby 1344 in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund De Appleby II 22 und ihre Mutter Agnes De Solney 19. Sie hatte mit Nicholas de Longford III mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1396 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Appleby è nata il nel 1344 nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Edmund De Appleby II, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Agnes De Solney, aveva 19 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Nicholas de Longford III. Morì nel 1396, nel Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Appleby(이)가 1344년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund De Appleby II(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes De Solney(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Nicholas de Longford III(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1396년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 애스h보우른에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
